title: Accessible Content Models, Landmarks, Headings, Links, Media, and Language · Guided Program Studio
summary: A civic-publishing pair incrementally builds WCAG 2.2 content and template conformance gate from original fixed content fixtures while retaining earlier Python, Git, HTML, CSS, URL, accessibility, security, determinism, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of WCAG 2.2 content and template conformance gate while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, a…
objectives: Enforce document language, unique main, named navigation, logical heading outline, bypass mechanism, title, and meaningful sequence.
Require meaningful link text, current-location evidence, multiple ways to find pages, breadcrumb semantics, and non-color state.
Validate image purpose, alt decision, captions, transcripts, audio description needs, decorative handling, and accessible fallback.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Landmark Heading Model
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop publishing team handles case 1766 during a large technical reference incremental rebuild. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate generated page structure and content on WCAG evidence and human reader tasks; preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions; then publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Enforce document language, unique main, named navigation, logical heading outline, bypass mechanism, title, and meaningful sequence.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Landmark Heading Model.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for enforce document language, unique main, named navigation, logical heading outline, bypass mechanism, title, and meaningful sequence. For Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Landmark Heading Model, keep Landmark Heading Model connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Accessibility Evidence
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: ssg-accessibility-evidence-xtv2nf" that uses case 1381 to gate generated page structure and content on WCAG evidence and human reader tasks. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, cache, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, CommonMark 0.31.2 fixtures, markdown-it-py 4.2.0, Jinja 3.1.6 strict autoescape, pytest 9.1.1, standards validators, real browsers, clean package installs, reproducible artifacts, staged deployment, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Accessibility Evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Accessibility Evidence into observable performance evidence. For Static-site publishing and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Accessibility Evidence, keep Accessibility Evidence connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-accessibility-evidence-xtv2nf
# Competency: ssg-accessibility-evidence.
# Case 1381: a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment.
# Operating constraint: retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity.
# Changed case: publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical.
def evidence_ssg_accessibility_evidence_xtv2nf(pages: int = 8, checked_pages: int = 8, structural_findings: int = 0, media_decisions_missing: int = 0, human_tasks_passed: int = 4) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_xtv2nf = "xtv2nf-fn2vtx-1381"
    assert evidence_variant_xtv2nf
    evidence_axes_xtv2nf = (
        "readerxtv2nf",
        "source1381",
        "parsefn2vtx",
        "routextv2nf1381",
        "artifact1381xtv2nf",
        "recoveryfn2vtx1381",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_xtv2nf)) == 6
    if pages < 1 or checked_pages < 0:
        return False, checked_pages, "page-count"
    if checked_pages != pages:
        return False, checked_pages, "accessibility-coverage"
    if structural_findings > 0 or media_decisions_missing > 0:
        return False, structural_findings + media_decisions_missing, "accessibility-finding"
    if human_tasks_passed < 3:
        return False, human_tasks_passed, "human-evidence"
    assert checked_pages == pages
    return True, human_tasks_passed, "accessible-content-model"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the transferred model · Workshop Accessibility Evidence

Build deterministic Python evidence to gate generated page structure and content on WCAG evidence and human reader tasks; reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval; then insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Combine structural checks, keyboard and zoom review, screen-reader tasks, content review, changed routes, and human ownership.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Combine structural checks, keyboard and zoom review, screen-reader tasks, content review, changed routes, and human ownership.
